annotations.label: Nilpotent group (lead)

anchor.snippet: a nilpotent group G is a group that has an upper central series that terminates with G

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Group.IsNilpotent G` exactly as the existence of `n` with `upperCentralSeries G n = ⊤`.

annotations.label: Nilpotent group via central series

anchor.snippet: The following are equivalent definitions for a nilpotent group G

annotations.note: Equivalent characterisations are given by `nilpotent_iff_finite_ascending_central_series`, `nilpotent_iff_finite_descending_central_series`, and `nilpotent_iff_lowerCentralSeries`.

annotations.label: Nilpotency class

anchor.snippet: the smallest n such that G has a central series of length n is called the nilpotency class of G

annotations.note: `Group.nilpotencyClass G` is the least `n` with `upperCentralSeries G n = ⊤`.

annotations.label: Nilpotency class equals length of lower/upper central series

anchor.snippet: the nilpotency class of G equals the length of the lower central series or upper central series

annotations.note: Length equality with the lower (resp. ascending) central series is `lowerCentralSeries_length_eq_nilpotencyClass` and `least_ascending_central_series_length_eq_nilpotencyClass`.

annotations.label: Trivial group and abelian groups by nilpotency class

anchor.snippet: the trivial group is the unique group of nilpotency class

annotations.note: Class 0 iff trivial is `nilpotencyClass_zero_iff_subsingleton`, and abelian groups have class ≤ 1 by `CommGroup.nilpotencyClass_le_one` with converse `commGroupOfNilpotencyClass`.

annotations.label: Abelian groups are nilpotent

anchor.snippet: every abelian group is nilpotent

annotations.note: The instance `CommGroup.isNilpotent` directly states every commutative group is nilpotent.

annotations.label: Quaternion group Q8 is nilpotent of class 2

anchor.snippet: consider the quaternion group Q 8 , which is a smallest non-abelian

annotations.note: `QuaternionGroup` is defined but no instance/lemma asserts Q8 is nilpotent of class 2 specifically.

annotations.label: Direct product of nilpotent groups

anchor.snippet: The direct product of two nilpotent groups is nilpotent

annotations.note: `Group.isNilpotent_prod` (and `Group.isNilpotent_pi` for finite products) provides this.

annotations.label: Finite p-groups are nilpotent

anchor.snippet: All finite p -groups are in fact nilpotent

annotations.note: `IsPGroup.isNilpotent` shows a finite p-group is nilpotent.

annotations.label: Finite nilpotent groups decompose as direct product of p-groups

anchor.snippet: every finite nilpotent group is the direct product of p -groups

annotations.note: Condition (5) of `Group.isNilpotent_of_finite_tfae` is precisely the Sylow direct-product isomorphism characterization.

annotations.label: Upper unitriangular matrices form a nilpotent group

anchor.snippet: The multiplicative group of upper unitriangular

annotations.note: No definition of the unitriangular matrix group or a nilpotency result for it was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Invertible upper triangular matrices are solvable but not nilpotent

anchor.snippet: The multiplicative group of invertible upper triangular

annotations.note: Mathlib has no group of invertible upper triangular matrices and no solvability/non-nilpotency theorem for it.

annotations.label: G/Z(G) abelian implies nilpotency class 2

anchor.snippet: Any nonabelian group G such that

annotations.note: `nilpotencyClass_eq_quotient_center_plus_one` combined with `CommGroup.nilpotencyClass_le_one` yields the statement, but it is not packaged as a single named lemma.

annotations.label: Orders k forcing nilpotency (OEIS A056867)

anchor.snippet: The natural numbers k for which any group of order k is nilpotent have been characterized

annotations.note: No characterization of nilpotent-forcing group orders is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Adjoint action and n-Engel groups

anchor.snippet: Nilpotent groups are called so because the "adjoint action" of any element is nilpotent

annotations.note: No group-theoretic notion of Engel/adjoint nilpotence is defined in Mathlib (the file `Mathlib/Algebra/Lie/Engel.lean` is Lie-algebraic).

annotations.label: n-Engel group

anchor.snippet: groups for which

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `nEngel`/`EngelGroup` definition for groups.

annotations.label: Abelian groups are 1-Engel

anchor.snippet: An abelian group is precisely one for which the adjoint action is not just nilpotent but trivial

annotations.note: Without an Engel definition for groups, this characterization is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Every nilpotent group is solvable

anchor.snippet: every nilpotent group is a solvable group with a relatively simple structure

annotations.note: `IsNilpotent.to_isSolvable` is the instance proving every nilpotent group is solvable.

annotations.label: Subgroups and homomorphic images of nilpotent groups

anchor.snippet: Every subgroup of a nilpotent group of class n is nilpotent of class at most n

annotations.note: Covered by `Subgroup.isNilpotent`, `Subgroup.nilpotencyClass_le`, `Group.nilpotent_of_surjective`, and `Group.nilpotencyClass_le_of_surjective`.

annotations.label: Equivalent characterizations of finite nilpotent groups
annotations.note: The TFAE list `Group.isNilpotent_of_finite_tfae` covers the standard equivalences (normalizer condition, normal Sylows, Sylow product).

annotations.label: Sylow subgroups of infinite nilpotent groups

anchor.snippet: if G is a nilpotent group, then every Sylow subgroup

annotations.note: Mathlib's Sylow-normality result for nilpotent groups is only stated in the finite TFAE; no infinite version was found.

annotations.label: Hypercentral groups share properties with nilpotent groups

anchor.snippet: Many properties of nilpotent groups are shared by hypercentral groups

annotations.note: Hypercentral groups (transfinite upper central series) are not defined in Mathlib.
